name: St. Louis Metro
description: The greater St. Louis metropolitan area encompassing the city and surrounding counties.

name: City of St. Louis
description: An independent city not part of any county, home to iconic landmarks and historic neighborhoods.

name: St. Louis County
description: The most populous county in Missouri, surrounding the city on three sides with diverse municipalities.

name: St. Charles County
description: A fast-growing county northwest of St. Louis along the Missouri River.

name: Soulard
description: One of St. Louis's oldest neighborhoods, known for its French street grid, lively bars, and Saturday farmers market.

name: Benton Park
description: A quiet residential neighborhood bordering Soulard, with beautiful Victorian homes surrounding a historic park.

name: Tower Grove
description: A collection of neighborhoods centered on Tower Grove Park, one of the finest Victorian parks in the country.

name: Lafayette Square
description: St. Louis's first planned neighborhood, featuring meticulously restored Italianate and Second Empire homes.

name: Central West End
description: An upscale urban neighborhood with sidewalk cafes, art galleries, and grand mansions along Maryland Avenue.

name: The Grove
description: A vibrant entertainment district on Manchester Avenue with a diverse mix of restaurants, bars, and live music venues.

name: Dogtown
description: A close-knit Irish-American neighborhood near Forest Park with strong community ties and modest brick bungalows.

name: Cherokee Street
description: A culturally diverse corridor known for its antique shops, Latin food, and thriving arts community.

name: Downtown St. Louis
description: The commercial and civic core of the city, anchored by the Gateway Arch, Busch Stadium, and the Gateway District.

name: Clayton
description: St. Louis County's seat of government and a premier address for corporate headquarters, fine dining, and upscale living.

name: Kirkwood
description: A charming historic suburb with a walkable downtown, Amtrak service, and tree-lined residential streets.

name: Webster Groves
description: An established inner suburb prized for its excellent schools, Victorian architecture, and small-town feel.

name: Chesterfield
description: A prosperous planned community in west St. Louis County featuring upscale retail, corporate parks, and new construction homes.

name: Ladue
description: One of Missouri's wealthiest municipalities, characterized by large lots, mature trees, and custom estate homes.

name: Creve Coeur
description: An affluent suburb home to several major corporations and a mix of mid-century and contemporary residences.

name: Des Peres
description: A well-maintained suburb known for West County Center, top-rated schools, and quiet residential streets.

name: Ballwin
description: A family-oriented suburb in west St. Louis County offering affordable housing and strong community programs.

name: Manchester
description: A compact suburb bisected by Manchester Road, offering easy highway access and established neighborhoods.

name: St. Charles
description: Missouri's first state capital, with a beautifully preserved Main Street lined with shops, restaurants, and historic buildings.

name: O'Fallon
description: The largest city in St. Charles County, a rapidly growing community with new developments and top-ranked schools.

name: Wentzville
description: One of the fastest-growing cities in Missouri, offering new construction at competitive prices with easy I-70 access.

name: Lake St. Louis
description: A planned community built around two lakes, offering waterfront living, golf, and resort-style amenities.

name: Benton Park West
description: An up-and-coming pocket west of Benton Park with affordable brick flats and a growing restaurant scene.

name: Tower Grove South
description: A densely residential area south of the park with strong community organizations and diverse housing stock.

name: Tower Grove East
description: A walkable neighborhood east of the park with an eclectic mix of eateries along Gravois Avenue.

name: Shaw
description: A historic neighborhood surrounding the Missouri Botanical Garden, prized for its brick homes and tight-knit community.

name: Midtown
description: The arts and entertainment corridor anchored by the Fox Theatre, Powell Hall, and Saint Louis University.

name: Cortex
description: A biotech and innovation district bridging the Central West End and the medical campus with new mixed-use development.

name: Forest Park Southeast
description: An emerging neighborhood on the southern edge of Forest Park with converted lofts and growing retail.

name: Downtown Clayton
description: The walkable urban core of Clayton, dense with high-rise condos, law firms, and acclaimed restaurants.

name: Old Town St. Charles
description: The historic heart of St. Charles, featuring brick-paved streets, Federal-style storefronts, and the Lewis & Clark heritage trail.

name: Historic Soulard
description: The oldest section of Soulard, with the highest concentration of pre-Civil War architecture in the city.

name: Chesterfield Valley
description: A flood-plain retail and residential district rebuilt after the 1993 floods, now home to major shopping and newer subdivisions.

name: Downtown Kirkwood
description: The vibrant walkable core of Kirkwood centered on Argonne Drive, with boutique shops, restaurants, and the train station.

name: Old Webster
description: The original core of Webster Groves surrounding Gore Avenue, with independent shops and some of the area's finest craftsman homes.
